rubric_en: The prime minister’s final few weeks sum him up
rubric_zh: 这位首相最后几周的作为概括了他这个人

paragraphs.en: To see how someone lived, examine how they died. The final few weeks of Sir Keir Starmer’s strange political life say much about the man.
paragraphs.zh: 若想看清一个人如何活过，就看他如何谢幕。基尔·斯塔默爵士这段奇特政治生涯的最后几周，很能说明其人。

paragraphs.en: Outstanding affairs have been put in order. Sir Keir hammered out an increase in defence spending of £15bn ($20bn) over four years. The bulk of it was paid for with cuts to capital-spending budgets. Roads will go unbuilt. Energy projects will be canned. You could call it a “sticking-plaster” approach to public investment. Bad for business investment. Bad for living standards. Bad for growth. And, in the end, bad for the public finances as well. Those words may sound familiar. They are Sir Keir’s, when attacking the Conservatives in 2023 for doing what he has now done: raiding capital budgets to pay for spending elsewhere.
paragraphs.zh: 未了之事已经一一安排妥当。基尔爵士敲定了在四年内增加150亿英镑（200亿美元）国防开支的方案。其中大部分资金来自削减资本支出预算。道路将不会开建。能源项目将被砍掉。你可以称之为公共投资上的“创可贴”做法。对商业投资不利。对生活水平不利。对增长不利。而且到头来，对公共财政同样不利。这些话听起来也许耳熟。它们正是基尔爵士在2023年攻击保守党时说过的话，当时保守党做了他如今也做的事：挪用资本预算，去支付其他领域的开支。

paragraphs.en: Others will have to deal with the inheritance. Even with the capital cuts, Sir Keir leaves behind about £5bn in unfunded spending for whoever is lucky enough to be Andy Burnham’s chancellor. When making way for someone in their own party, a prime minister should act as a sin-eater, choking down the consequences of the errors made in government, allowing their successor the benefit of purity. When it comes to defence spending, Sir Keir is less sin-eating than sin-chewing-and-spitting-into-a-napkin.
paragraphs.zh: 这笔遗产将由别人来收拾。即便削减了资本开支，基尔爵士仍给那位有幸成为安迪·伯纳姆财政大臣的人留下了约50亿英镑没有资金来源的支出。首相若是给本党内的人让路，就应当扮演“替罪羊”的角色，咽下政府犯错带来的后果，让继任者得以保持清白。就国防开支而言，基尔爵士与其说是在吞罪，不如说是把罪嚼了嚼又吐进餐巾纸里。

paragraphs.en: There was a final trip to Paris. Where else? Sir Keir was hosted by Emmanuel Macron at a meeting of the “coalition of the willing” of Ukraine allies. The French president led plaudits and awarded him the Légion d’honneur as a sign of the improved relations between both countries. If patriotism is the last refuge of a scoundrel, foreign policy is the last refuge of an unsuccessful prime minister. Sir Keir, more than any recent occupant, loved it, attracting plaudits for deft diplomacy even when pilloried at home.
paragraphs.zh: 最后一次出访去了巴黎。还能去哪儿呢？基尔爵士受到埃马纽埃尔·马克龙接待，出席乌克兰盟友“志愿联盟”会议。法国总统带头致以赞誉，并授予他荣誉军团勋章，以示两国关系改善。如果说爱国主义是无赖最后的避难所，那么外交政策就是不成功首相最后的避难所。基尔爵士比近来的任何一位唐宁街主人都更钟爱外交，即便在国内遭到猛烈抨击，也能凭借娴熟外交赢得喝彩。

paragraphs.en: Beneath the diplomatic pomp, personal relationships matter less than many assume. Where Britain was useful, it was welcomed. The coalition of the willing would have been far less potent without Britain, which is still one of only two serious hard powers in western Europe. Where Britain was weak, it was bullied. Under Sir Keir, Britain struggled to win concessions from the eu—often because of the man handing over the Légion d’honneur to a beaming prime minister.
paragraphs.zh: 在外交排场之下，个人关系并没有许多人以为的那么重要。英国有用之处，就受到欢迎。若没有英国，志愿联盟的力量会小得多，因为英国仍是西欧仅有的两个严肃硬实力国家之一。英国软弱之处，就会受人欺压。在基尔爵士任内，英国很难从欧盟那里争取到让步，往往正是因为那位把荣誉军团勋章交给笑容满面的首相的人。

paragraphs.en: Back in London, Sir Keir gathered the people he always spoke for: victims. He saw politics through their eyes. On a warm afternoon on July 14th, he welcomed a collection of them to the Downing Street garden: one mother whose son was killed by a ninja sword, and another whose child perished in the Manchester Arena attacks in 2017. Also in attendance were parents of those killed in the Hillsborough disaster, in which 97 Liverpool Football Club fans died due to police incompetence before being slandered by a malevolent state.
paragraphs.zh: 回到伦敦后，基尔爵士召集了他始终代言的人群：受害者。他透过他们的眼睛看政治。7月14日一个温暖的下午，他在唐宁街花园欢迎了一批受害者：一位母亲的儿子死于忍者刀之下，另一位母亲的孩子在2017年曼彻斯特体育馆袭击中丧生。到场的还有希尔斯堡惨案遇难者的父母；那场灾难中，97名利物浦足球俱乐部球迷因警方无能而死亡，随后又遭一个恶意国家机器的污蔑。

paragraphs.en: Thanks to Sir Keir’s government, each now has a law named after their tragedies. For a man with few guiding principles, victims provided a moral map. The job of the Labour Party was “to look directly into the eyes of their suffering”, said Sir Keir. It is a humane response. But governing is not a humane job. Was banning ninja swords, already illegal to carry in public, a good use of legislative time? Is it proportionate to require a church hall to have an anti-terror plan? Will the “duty of candour” that the Hillsborough law introduces lead to openness, or to public servants becoming strategically uninformed about brewing trouble? A government must be clear-eyed, and grief makes people blind.
paragraphs.zh: 由于基尔爵士政府的作为，如今每一场悲剧都有了一部以其命名的法律。对于一个几乎没有指导原则的人来说，受害者提供了一张道德地图。基尔爵士说，工党的职责是“直视他们苦难的眼睛”。这是一种富有人性的回应。但治理国家并不是一份富有人性的工作。禁止忍者刀，是否值得占用立法时间，何况这种刀本来就已禁止在公共场所携带？要求一座教堂大厅制定反恐计划，是否合乎比例？希尔斯堡法引入的“坦诚义务”，究竟会带来公开透明，还是会让公职人员在麻烦酝酿时策略性地保持不知情？政府必须眼明心亮，而悲痛会让人失明。

paragraphs.en: Cabinet ministers gathered for the final time on July 15th to send off a prime minister regarded by all but a few of them with contempt. They gave him a carriage clock—the symbol of an unthinking retirement gift. Still, it was a serene end. Good prime ministers tend to enjoy messy departures, argued Andrew Adonis, a former Labour minister, in a note to his then soon-to-be-departing boss, Sir Tony Blair. By contrast, Sir Keir has enjoyed a form of political euthanasia, put out of his misery by people he once considered friends. It is a dignity he did not extend to the public after he failed to pass a euthanasia bill, although it was one of the few things in which he sincerely believed.
paragraphs.zh: 7月15日，内阁大臣们最后一次聚集，为一位除寥寥数人外几乎人人鄙视的首相送行。他们送给他一座马车钟，这是不假思索的退休礼物的象征。不过，这仍是一个平静的结局。前工党大臣安德鲁·阿多尼斯曾在写给当时即将离任的上司托尼·布莱尔爵士的一份备忘录中说，优秀的首相往往会有混乱的离场。相比之下，基尔爵士享受了一种政治安乐死，被他曾经视为朋友的人结束了痛苦。这份体面，他在未能通过安乐死法案后并没有给予公众，尽管那是他少数真心相信的事情之一。

paragraphs.en: Since Sir Keir’s fate has been settled officially for nearly a month (and unofficially for far longer), the prime minister has had time to offer interviews about his legacy. What policy does he want to be remembered for? Why, scrapping the two-child limit on benefits, of course, which lifted hundreds of thousands of children out of poverty. It is the sort of thing Labour should do. Unmentioned was the fact Sir Keir fought such a move for almost two years, even sacking seven mps for calling for it. It could have been announced on day one. Instead, it happened on day 509.
paragraphs.zh: 由于基尔爵士的命运已正式尘埃落定近一个月（非正式地说则早得多），这位首相有时间接受采访，谈论自己的政治遗产。他希望哪项政策被人铭记？当然是取消福利中的二孩上限，因为这让数十万儿童摆脱了贫困。这是工党本该做的事。没有提到的是，基尔爵士为阻止此举抗拒了将近两年，甚至因有7名议员呼吁这样做而将他们开除。这本可以在第一天宣布。结果却发生在第509天。

paragraphs.en: Sir Keir would have spent his final few moments in power 5,500km away in New Jersey, had Argentina not ended England’s hopes in the World Cup. By a remarkable coincidence, the football-mad prime minister had picked the day after the final as the earliest possible moment for the handover. Sir Keir began his reign in a furore about free football tickets he enjoyed while leader of the opposition. Why change the habit? England in a World Cup final would have been more justifiable than Arsenal away at Newcastle.
paragraphs.zh: 如果不是阿根廷终结了英格兰在世界杯上的希望，基尔爵士本会在离权力交接还有最后片刻时，身处5500公里外的新泽西。极为巧合的是，这位痴迷足球的首相把决赛次日选定为最早可能的交接时刻。基尔爵士执政伊始，就因担任反对党领袖期间享受免费足球票而陷入风波。何必改变这个习惯呢？英格兰参加世界杯决赛，总比阿森纳客场对阵纽卡斯尔更说得过去。

paragraphs.en: Some prime ministers work to the last second. Others simply let death take them. In one of those final interviews, Sir Keir was asked for his proudest moment. He chose walking up Downing Street the day after the general election. It was winning for the sake of winning. Why he wanted the job, Britain never quite learned. Sir Keir composed his own epitaph before he had even come to power, during one of Labour’s interminable internal rows: “There is no such thing as Starmerism, and there never will be.” How right he was.■
paragraphs.zh: 有些首相工作到最后一秒。另一些则只是任由死亡带走自己。在最后几次采访中的一次，基尔爵士被问及最自豪的时刻。他选择的是大选次日走上唐宁街的那一刻。那是为了胜利而胜利。英国始终没有真正明白，他为什么想要这份工作。基尔爵士甚至还没上台，就已经在工党没完没了的一场内部争吵中为自己写好了墓志铭：“不存在所谓斯塔默主义，也永远不会有。”他说得多么正确。■

paragraphs.en: Subscribers to The Economist can sign up to our Opinion newsletter, which brings together the best of our leaders, columns, guest essays and reader correspondence.
paragraphs.zh: 《经济学人》的订阅用户可以订阅我们的 Opinion 新闻通讯，其中汇集了我们最优秀的社论、专栏、客座文章和读者来信。
